an3.70:1.2: that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī in the Eastern Monastery, the palace of Migāra’s mother.
an3.70:1.3: Now at that time—it being the uposatha day—Visākhā, Migāra’s mother, went to the Blessed One in the middle of the day and, on arrival, having bowed down to him, sat to one side.
an3.70:1.4: As she was sitting there the Blessed One said to her, “Well now, Visākhā, why are you coming in the middle of the day?”
an3.70:1.5: “Today I am observing the uposatha, lord.”
an3.70:2.3: The uposatha of a cowherd, the uposatha of the Jains, and the uposatha of the noble ones.
an3.70:2.4: “And what is the uposatha of a cowherd?
an3.70:2.5: Just as when a cowherd returns the cattle to their owners in the late afternoon, he reflects:
an3.70:2.6: ‘Today the cattle wandered to that spot and this, drank at this spot and that;
an3.70:2.7: tomorrow they will wander to that spot and this, will drink at this spot and that’;
an3.70:2.8: in the same way, there is the case where a certain person observing the uposatha reflects,
an3.70:2.9: ‘Today I ate this sort of non-staple food and that sort of staple food.
an3.70:2.10: Tomorrow I will eat that sort of non-staple food and this sort of staple food.’
an3.70:2.11: He spends the day with an awareness imbued with that covetousness, with that greed.
an3.70:2.12: Such is the uposatha of a cowherd, Visākhā.
an3.70:2.13: When this uposatha of a cowherd is undertaken, it is not of great fruit or great benefit, not of great glory or great radiance.
an3.70:3.1: “And what is the uposatha of the Jains?
an3.70:3.2: There are the contemplatives called the Nigaṇṭhas [Jains].
an3.70:3.3: They get their disciple to undertake the following practice:
an3.70:3.4: ‘Here, my good man. Lay down the rod with regard to beings who live more than 100 leagues to the east…
an3.70:3.5: more than 100 leagues to the west…
an3.70:3.6: more than 100 leagues to the north…
an3.70:3.7: more than 100 leagues to the south.’
an3.70:3.8: Thus they get the disciple to undertake kindness & sympathy to some beings, but not to others.
an3.70:3.9: “On the uposatha day, they get their disciple to undertake the following practice:
an3.70:3.10: ‘Here, my good man. Having stripped off all your clothing, say this:
an3.70:3.11: “I am nothing by anything or of anything. Thus there is nothing by anything or of anything that is mine.”’
an3.70:3.12: Yet in spite of that, his parents know of him that
an3.70:3.14: And he knows of them that
an3.70:3.16: His wives & children know of him that
an3.70:3.18: And he knows of them that
an3.70:3.20: His workers & slaves know of him that
an3.70:3.22: And he knows of them that
an3.70:3.24: Thus at a time when he should be persuaded to undertake truthfulness,
an3.70:3.25: he is persuaded to undertake falsehood.
an3.70:3.26: At the end of the night, he resumes the consumption of his belongings, even though they aren’t given back to him.
an3.70:3.27: This counts as stealing, I tell you.
an3.70:3.28: Such is the uposatha of the Jains, Visākhā.
an3.70:3.29: When this uposatha of the Jains is undertaken, it is not of great fruit or great benefit, not of great glory or great radiance.
an3.70:4.1: “And what is the uposatha of the noble ones?
an3.70:4.2: It is the cleansing of the defiled mind through the proper technique.
an3.70:4.3: And how is the defiled mind cleansed through the proper technique?
an3.70:4.4: “There is the case where a disciple of the noble ones recollects the Tathāgata, thus:
an3.70:4.5: ‘Indeed, the Blessed One is worthy & rightly self-awakened, consummate in clear-knowing & conduct, Well-Gone, an expert with regard to the cosmos, unexcelled trainer of people fit to be tamed, teacher of devas & human beings, awakened, blessed.’
an3.70:4.6: As he is recollecting the Tathāgata, his mind is calmed, and joy arises; the defilements of his mind are abandoned, just as when the head is cleansed through the proper technique.
an3.70:5.1: And how is the head cleansed through the proper technique?
an3.70:5.2: Through the use of cosmetic paste & clay & the appropriate human effort.
an3.70:5.3: This is how the head is cleansed through the proper technique.
an3.70:6.1: In the same way, the defiled mind is cleansed through the proper technique.
an3.70:6.2: And how is the defiled mind cleansed through the proper technique?
an3.70:6.3: There is the case where the disciple of the noble ones recollects the Tathāgata.…
an3.70:6.4: As he is recollecting the Tathāgata, his mind is cleansed, and joy arises; the defilements of his mind are abandoned.
an3.70:6.5: He is thus called a disciple of the noble ones undertaking the Brahmā-uposatha.
an3.70:6.6: He lives with Brahmā [= the Buddha]. It is owing to Brahmā that his mind is calmed, that joy arises, and that whatever defilements there are in his mind are abandoned.
an3.70:6.7: This is how the mind is cleansed through the proper technique.
an3.70:7.1: “(Again, the uposatha of the noble ones) is the cleansing of the defiled mind through the proper technique.
an3.70:7.2: And how is the defiled mind cleansed through the proper technique?
an3.70:7.3: “There is the case where the disciple of the noble ones recollects the Dhamma, thus:
an3.70:7.4: ‘The Dhamma is well taught by the Blessed One, to be seen here & now, timeless, inviting verification, pertinent, to be experienced by the observant for themselves.’
an3.70:7.5: As he is recollecting the Dhamma, his mind is calmed, and joy arises; the defilements of his mind are abandoned, just as when the body is cleansed through the proper technique.
an3.70:8.1: And how is the body cleansed through the proper technique?
an3.70:8.2: Through the use of scouring balls & bath powder & the appropriate human effort.
an3.70:8.3: This is how the body is cleansed through the proper technique.
an3.70:8.4: In the same way, the defiled mind is cleansed through the proper technique.
an3.70:9.1: And how is the defiled mind cleansed through the proper technique?
an3.70:9.2: There is the case where the disciple of the noble ones recollects the Dhamma.…
an3.70:9.4: As he is recollecting the Dhamma, his mind is cleansed, and joy arises; the defilements of his mind are abandoned.
an3.70:9.5: He is thus called a disciple of the noble ones undertaking the Dhamma-uposatha. He lives with Dhamma. It is owing to Dhamma that his mind is calmed, that joy arises, and that whatever defilements there are in his mind are abandoned.
an3.70:9.6: This is how the mind is cleansed through the proper technique.
an3.70:10.1: “(Again, the uposatha of the noble ones) is the cleansing of the defiled mind through the proper technique.
an3.70:10.2: And how is the defiled mind cleansed through the proper technique?
an3.70:10.3: “There is the case where the disciple of the noble ones recollects the Saṅgha, thus:
an3.70:10.4: ‘The Saṅgha of the Blessed One’s disciples who have practiced well… who have practiced straight-forwardly… who have practiced methodically… who have practiced masterfully—in other words, the four types (of noble disciples) when taken as pairs, the eight when taken as individual types—they are the Saṅgha of the Blessed One’s disciples: deserving of gifts, deserving of hospitality, deserving of offerings, deserving of respect, the incomparable field of merit for the world.’
an3.70:10.5: As he is recollecting the Saṅgha, his mind is calmed, and joy arises; the defilements of his mind are abandoned, just as when clothing is cleansed through the proper technique.
an3.70:11.1: And how is clothing cleansed through the proper technique?
an3.70:11.2: Through the use of salt earth & lye & cow dung & the appropriate human effort.
an3.70:11.3: This is how clothing is cleansed through the proper technique.
an3.70:11.4: In the same way, the defiled mind is cleansed through the proper technique.
an3.70:12.1: And how is the defiled mind cleansed through the proper technique?
an3.70:12.2: There is the case where the disciple of the noble ones recollects the Saṅgha.…
an3.70:12.4: As he is recollecting the Saṅgha, his mind is cleansed, and joy arises; the defilements of his mind are abandoned.
an3.70:12.5: He is thus called a disciple of the noble ones undertaking the Saṅgha-uposatha. He lives with the Saṅgha. It is owing to the Saṅgha that his mind is calmed, that joy arises, and that whatever defilements there are in his mind are abandoned.
an3.70:12.6: This is how the mind is cleansed through the proper technique.
an3.70:13.1: “(Again, the uposatha of the noble ones) is the cleansing of the defiled mind through the proper technique.
an3.70:13.2: And how is the defiled mind cleansed through the proper technique?
an3.70:13.3: “There is the case where the disciple of the noble ones recollects his own virtues, thus:
an3.70:13.4: ‘(They are) untorn, unbroken, unspotted, unsplattered, liberating, praised by the observant, ungrasped at, conducive to concentration.’ As he is recollecting virtue, his mind is calmed, and joy arises; the defilements of his mind are abandoned, just as when a mirror is cleansed through the proper technique.
an3.70:14.1: And how is a mirror cleansed through the proper technique?
an3.70:14.2: Through the use of oil & ashes & chamois & the appropriate human effort.
an3.70:14.3: This is how a mirror is cleansed through the proper technique.
an3.70:14.4: In the same way, the defiled mind is cleansed through the proper technique.
an3.70:15.1: And how is the defiled mind cleansed through the proper technique?
an3.70:15.2: There is the case where the disciple of the noble ones recollects his own virtues.…
an3.70:15.3: As he is recollecting virtue, his mind is cleansed, and joy arises; the defilements of his mind are abandoned.
an3.70:15.4: He is thus called a disciple of the noble ones undertaking the virtue-uposatha. He lives with virtue. It is owing to virtue that his mind is calmed, that joy arises, and that whatever defilements there are in his mind are abandoned.
an3.70:15.5: This is how the mind is cleansed through the proper technique.
an3.70:16.1: “(Again, the uposatha of the noble ones) is the cleansing of the defiled mind through the proper technique.
an3.70:16.2: And how is the defiled mind cleansed through the proper technique?
an3.70:16.3: “There is the case where the disciple of the noble ones recollects the devas, thus:
an3.70:16.4: ‘There are the Devas of the Four Great Kings, the Devas of the Thirty-three, the Devas of the Hours, the Contented Devas, the Devas Delighting in Creation, the Devas [Muses?] Wielding Power over the Creations of Others, the Devas of Brahmā’s retinue, the devas beyond them.
an3.70:16.5: Whatever conviction they were endowed with that—when falling away from this life—they re-arose there, the same sort of conviction is present in me as well.
an3.70:16.6: Whatever virtue they were endowed with that—when falling away from this life—they re-arose there, the same sort of virtue is present in me as well.
an3.70:16.7: Whatever learning they were endowed with that—when falling away from this life—they re-arose there, the same sort of learning is present in me as well.
an3.70:16.8: Whatever generosity they were endowed with that—when falling away from this life—they re-arose there, the same sort of generosity is present in me as well.
an3.70:16.9: Whatever discernment they were endowed with that—when falling away from this life—they re-arose there, the same sort of discernment is present in me as well.’
an3.70:16.10: As he is recollecting the devas, his mind is calmed, and joy arises; the defilements of his mind are abandoned, just as when gold is cleansed through the proper technique.
an3.70:17.1: And how is gold cleansed through the proper technique?
an3.70:17.2: Through the use of a furnace, salt earth, red chalk, a blow-pipe, tongs, & the appropriate human effort.
an3.70:17.3: This is how gold is cleansed through the proper technique.
an3.70:17.4: In the same way, the defiled mind is cleansed through the proper technique.
an3.70:18.1: And how is the defiled mind cleansed through the proper technique?
an3.70:18.2: There is the case where the disciple of the noble ones recollects the devas.…
an3.70:18.9: As he is recollecting the devas, his mind is cleansed, and joy arises; the defilements of his mind are abandoned.
an3.70:18.10: He is thus called a disciple of the noble ones undertaking the Deva-uposatha. He lives with the devas. It is owing to the devas that his mind is calmed, that joy arises, and that whatever defilements there are in his mind are abandoned.
an3.70:18.11: This is how the mind is cleansed through the proper technique.
an3.70:19.1: “And further, the disciple of the noble ones reflects thus:
an3.70:19.2: ‘As long as they live, the arahants—abandoning the taking of life—abstain from the taking of life. They dwell with their rod laid down, their knife laid down, scrupulous, merciful, compassionate for the welfare of all living beings.
an3.70:19.3: Today I too, for this day & night—abandoning the taking of life—abstain from the taking of life. I dwell with my rod laid down, my knife laid down, scrupulous, merciful, compassionate for the welfare of all living beings.
an3.70:19.4: By means of this factor I emulate the arahants, and my uposatha will be observed.
an3.70:20.1: “‘As long as they live, the arahants—abandoning the taking of what is not given—abstain from taking what is not given. They take only what is given, accept only what is given, live not by stealth but by means of a self that has become pure.
an3.70:20.2: Today I too, for this day & night—abandoning the taking of what is not given—abstain from taking what is not given. I take only what is given, accept only what is given, live not by stealth but by means of a self that has become pure.
an3.70:20.3: By means of this factor I emulate the arahants, and my uposatha will be observed.
an3.70:21.1: “‘As long as they live, the arahants—abandoning uncelibacy—live a celibate life, aloof, refraining from the sexual act that is the villager’s way.
an3.70:21.2: Today I too, for this day & night—abandoning uncelibacy—live a celibate life, aloof, refraining from the sexual act that is the villager’s way.
an3.70:21.3: By means of this factor I emulate the arahants, and my uposatha will be observed.
an3.70:22.1: “‘As long as they live, the arahants—abandoning the telling of lies—abstain from telling lies. They speak the truth, hold to the truth, are firm, reliable, no deceivers of the world.
an3.70:22.2: Today I too, for this day & night—abandoning the telling of lies—abstain from telling lies. I speak the truth, hold to the truth, am firm, reliable, no deceiver of the world.
an3.70:22.3: By means of this factor I emulate the arahants, and my uposatha will be observed.
an3.70:23.1: “‘As long as they live, the arahants—abandoning fermented & distilled liquors that cause heedlessness—abstain from fermented & distilled liquors that cause heedlessness.
an3.70:23.2: Today I too, for this day & night—abandoning fermented & distilled liquors that cause heedlessness—abstain from fermented & distilled liquors that cause heedlessness.
an3.70:23.3: By means of this factor I emulate the arahants, and my uposatha will be observed.
an3.70:24.1: “‘As long as they live, the arahants live on one meal a day, abstaining from food at night, refraining from food at the wrong time of day [from noon until dawn].
an3.70:24.2: Today I too, for this day & night, live on one meal, abstaining from food at night, refraining from food at the wrong time of day.
an3.70:24.3: By means of this factor I emulate the arahants, and my uposatha will be observed.
an3.70:25.1: “‘As long as they live, the arahants abstain from dancing, singing, music, watching shows, wearing garlands, beautifying themselves with perfumes & cosmetics.
an3.70:25.2: Today I too, for this day & night, abstain from dancing, singing, music, watching shows, wearing garlands, beautifying myself with perfumes & cosmetics.
an3.70:25.3: By means of this factor I emulate the arahants, and my uposatha will be observed.
an3.70:26.1: “‘As long as they live, the arahants—abandoning high & imposing seats & beds—abstain from high & imposing seats & beds. They make low beds, on a pallet or a spread of straw.
an3.70:26.2: Today I too, for this day & night—abandoning high & imposing seats & beds—abstain from high & imposing seats & beds. I make a low bed, on a pallet or a spread of straw.’
an3.70:27.1: “Such is the uposatha of the noble ones, Visākhā.
an3.70:27.2: When this uposatha of the noble ones is undertaken, it is of great fruit & great benefit, of great glory & great radiance.
an3.70:28.1: And how is it of great fruit & great benefit, of great glory & great radiance?
an3.70:28.2: “Suppose that one were to exercise kingship, rule, & sovereignty over these sixteen great lands replete with the seven treasures, i.e.,
an3.70:28.3: over the Aṅgas, Magadhans, Kasis, Kosalans, Vajjians, Mallans, Cetis, Vaṁsās, Kurus, Pañcālas, Macchas, Surasenas, Assakas, Avantis, Gandhārans, & Kambojans: It would not be worth one-sixteenth of this uposatha endowed with eight factors.
an3.70:28.5: Kingship over human beings is a meager thing when compared with heavenly bliss.
an3.70:29.1: “Fifty human years are equal to one day & night among the Devas of the Four Great Kings.
an3.70:29.2: Thirty such days & nights make a month.
an3.70:29.3: Twelve such months make a year.
an3.70:29.4: Five hundred such heavenly years constitute the life-span among the Devas of the Four Great Kings.
an3.70:29.5: Now, it is possible that a certain man or woman—from having observed this uposatha endowed with eight factors—on the break-up of the body, after death, might be reborn among the Devas of the Four Great Kings.
an3.70:29.6: It was in reference to this that it was said,
an3.70:29.7: ‘Kingship over human beings is a meager thing when compared with heavenly bliss.’
an3.70:30.1: “A human century is equal to one day & night among the Devas of the Thirty-Three.
an3.70:30.2: Thirty such days & nights make a month…
an3.70:30.4: One thousand such heavenly years constitute the life-span among the Devas of the Thirty-three.
an3.70:30.5: Now, it is possible that a certain man or woman—from having observed this uposatha endowed with eight factors—on the break-up of the body, after death, might be reborn among the Devas of the Thirty-three.
an3.70:30.6: It was in reference to this that it was said,
an3.70:30.7: ‘Kingship over human beings is a meager thing when compared with heavenly bliss.’
an3.70:31.1: “Two human centuries are equal to one day & night among the Devas of the Hours…
an3.70:31.4: Two thousand such heavenly years constitute the life-span among the Devas of the Hours…
an3.70:32.1: “Four human centuries are equal to one day & night among the Contented Devas…
an3.70:32.4: Four thousand such heavenly years constitute the life-span among the Contented Devas…
an3.70:33.1: Eight human centuries is equal to one day & night among the Devas Delighting in Creation…
an3.70:33.4: Eight thousand such heavenly years constitute the life-span among the Devas who Delight in Creation…
an3.70:34.1: Sixteen human centuries are equal to one day & night among the Devas Wielding Power over the Creations of Others.
an3.70:34.2: Thirty such days & nights make a month.
an3.70:34.3: Twelve such months make a year.
an3.70:34.4: Sixteen thousand such heavenly years constitute the life-span among the Devas Wielding Power over the Creations of Others.
an3.70:34.5: Now, it is possible that a certain man or woman—from having observed this uposatha endowed with eight factors—on the break-up of the body, after death, might be reborn among the Devas Wielding Power over the Creations of Others.
an3.70:34.6: It was in reference to this that it was said,
an3.70:34.7: ‘Kingship over human beings is a meager thing when compared with heavenly bliss.’”
an3.70:35.1: One should not kill a being or take what is not given;
an3.70:35.2: should not tell a lie or be a drinker of strong drink;
an3.70:35.3: should abstain from uncelibacy, the sexual act;
an3.70:35.4: should not eat at night, the wrong time of day;
an3.70:36.1: should not wear a garland or use a scent;
an3.70:36.2: should sleep on a pallet, a mat spread on the ground—
an3.70:36.4: has been proclaimed by the Awakened One to lead to the end of suffering & stress.
an3.70:37.1: The moon & sun, both fair to see,
an3.70:37.3: & scattering darkness as they move through space,
an3.70:37.4: brighten the sky, illumining the quarters.
an3.70:38.1: Within their range is found wealth:
an3.70:39.1: Yet they—like the light of all stars
an3.70:39.3: aren’t worth one sixteenth of the eight-factored uposatha.
an3.70:40.1: So whoever—man or woman—is endowed with the virtues
an3.70:40.3: having done meritorious deeds, productive of bliss,
an3.70:40.4: beyond reproach, goes to the heavenly state.
